titleStmt.title: Letter from M. Curry to Nancy O'Rahilly, 15 June, 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: M. Curry

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This is a letter of condolence to Nancy O'Rahilly (1875-1961). This letter expresses the sympathy of 'all the Sisters & many boarders'. The writer thinks often and prays for Mrs. O'Rahilly and her deceased husband. The writer asks for a supply of mortuary cards and hopes the children are a comfort to her.She was a member of the Provisional Council of Cumann na mBan at the founding in 1914 and the wife of Michael Joseph O'Rahilly, self-described as 'The' O'Rahilly (1875-1916). The O'Rahilly fought in the Easter Rising and was shot in Parnell/Moore Street on 28 April 1916 and died some time later. Nancy was pregnant at the time of her husband's death – the child was born three months later.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
St Josephs June 15 1916 Dear Mrs Rahilly I hope by this
our Good Lord has
given you resignation
in your great trouble
I have very often thought
of you, & prayed for you
& the dear dead R.I.P.
You have the sympathy
of all the Sisters & many boarders.
I wrote a few lines to Mrs. Humphreys
some time since & asked her
to convey my sympathy to you,
did not know your address
till seeing advertisement about
poor Michael's ring etc. Our Dear
Lord be good to him. I suppose
you got no tidings of ring. Will you please spare me
as many of his M. cards as
possible, a few of the Sisters
have asked me to try & get
them one, & a few of the
Lady Boarders. Then I want one
for Mrs. Morris & self. I trust the
children are well. God comfort you
With deepest sympathy
dear Mrs. O'Rahilly
Yours Sincerely M.Curry
